content.text: The fight starts when you say it does.
The Shadow's defense isn't Evasion and isn't stealth — it's tempo: strike before they're awake, be gone before they swing. The Dancer dodges dice. The Shadow dodges turns.
Two branches split the verb. Viper strikes before they wake. Ricochet is the pinball: the hit buys the run.
Per after-action audit, several opposing personnel concluded their contracts without logging a single action. Their employer has been invoiced for the unused rounds.

content.stat_lean: None printed — the engine is the activation order itself. Initiative and slot-claiming are Reflex's market (see Turn, AP & Reactions); Slit Timing buys past it.
content.theme: Tempo: the Opener, the hit that buys the run, and the kiting tax.
content.design: Strike before they're awake; be gone before they swing.

content.text: The Opener

Opener: an attack against an enemy that has not yet activated this round. Alternating activations make this a real, trackable state every round (initiative — see Turn, AP & Reactions), and the Shadow is the tree that weaponizes the order itself.

Uptime, simmed across four-round fights: 90% of attacks are Openers with Slit Timing; 64% from a random slot. The currency is strongest in round one and decays as the fight wears on — the Shadow front-loads, then earns its keep with Patient Knife and tempo sabotage. That decay is the tree's bill: round one is rich; round four is honest work.

Sneaking stays the Stealth skill's job, and stealth is a phase, not a state — it ends at initiative, and its combat payoff is the ambush round (see Bands & Movement). The ambush round is Opener heaven, but the Shadow needs neither: it cares about one thing only — whether you've had your turn yet.
The Kiting Tax
The pinball's passive damage: an enemy forced to chase one band per turn loses attacks.
The pinball hard-unplugs heavy weapons. The doctrine triangle, printed across three trees: Heavies lose to Shadow. Shadow loses to Fortress. Fortress loses to volume and range.
Counterplay, Printed — the Bumper Duel
A kitted Fortress beats a kitted Shadow at the door. The entry trigger is the killer: the pinball's protection keys to after the hit, and Gatekeeper collects before it. Rung by rung:

rows: None — the hit hasn't happened yet
Slipstream-style tech is the Dancer's, not yours
Hit and Fade dodges the trigger, not the toll

content.text: The Shadow's real counterplay to a Fortress is the oldest one: don't play their game. There are other doors and softer targets.
The Catalog
Install costs follow the standard ladder (Trees Overview). Fifteen augments; Tier 5 means choosing ten. Capstones are marked.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The Shadow doc self-reports “16 augments + entry,” but enumerates 14 + entry — 15 total, below the ~20-augment density standard, with no expansion pack printed. Tier 5's “choose 10” here means 10 of 15.
Viper Branch — Strike Before They Wake

augments.name: Slit Timing
augments.effect: You may always claim your side's first activation slot. The engine perk — 90% Opener uptime is bought here.

augments.name: Cold Read
augments.effect: Your Opener attacks deal +1 damage per die. Doubles knife output against Standard armor (2.6 to 5.3 per turn); Plate stays a doctrine wall.

augments.name: Rattlesnake
augments.effect: Enemies damaged by your Opener attacks are Rattled until the end of the round (the condition — see Conditions). Waking up hurt makes you sloppy.

augments.name: Double Cold
augments.effect: Once per round, killing an un-activated enemy grants one free attack against another un-activated enemy in reach.

augments.name: Patient Knife
augments.effect: Once per enemy per fight, your first attack against them counts as an Opener even after they've activated. Everyone's asleep once, if you wait.

augments.name: Sandman Toxin
augments.effect: Your Opener riders — conditions — apply even on a graze. A priced exception to the graze rule (see Graze & Crits).

augments.name: Round Zero
augments.effect: Capstone. At the start of combat, before any activation, take one 2 AP action. The fight starts when you say it does.

content.text: Ricochet Branch — The Pinball

augments.name: Glancing Orbit
augments.effect: +1 Evasion while in an enemy's Adjacent cluster you entered this turn. You arrived moving.

augments.name: Hit and Fade
augments.effect: After you hit with a melee attack, your next band move this turn doesn't trigger reaction attacks. The hit buys the run.

augments.name: Springload Calves
augments.effect: If you've hit an enemy this turn, your first band move afterward costs 0 AP.

augments.name: Pinball Path
augments.effect: When you kill an enemy, immediately move one band for free, with no reaction triggers.

augments.name: Carom Strike
augments.effect: 2 AP: move one band, attack, move one band; neither move triggers reactions. The signature, packaged.

augments.name: Restless Frame
augments.effect: Enemy reaction attacks against you cost +1 RP. Taxing the toll collector.

augments.name: Perpetual Motion
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per turn, hitting an enemy refunds 1 AP of movement, and your band moves never provoke reactions from enemies you've damaged this fight. The ball doesn't stop.

content.text: Pairings
+Dancer: Opener into flurry is the obvious cocktail — but Shadow's bonuses die when the room wakes up, and the Dancer's Evasion penalty starts when the swinging starts.
+Fury (Carnage): Pinball Path plus Cleave chains are a kill-conga; Wrath still starves on a hit-and-run diet.
+Fortress: the comedy build — the bumper that pinballs. Legal, confused, occasionally brilliant in doorway-rich maps.
Punctuality review: contractor consistently begins engagements before they are scheduled to start. This is the only acceptable form of early.
